Cyrtochilum amphiglottii is a type of terrestrial orchid, which means it grows in the ground rather than in trees. This orchid is native to South America, specifically in Colombia. intro: Cyrtochilum amphiglottii, commonly known as the amphiglottis orchid, is a species of orchid that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. Traditionally, the plant is used in folk medicine due to its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, making it useful in treating various conditions such as wounds, infections, and respiratory issues.
